note: Roadmap #13 Phase 3 — substitute for Mitchell IHS (Springer paywall). Open sources only: FRED (US, NBER Macrohistory + BLS, CC0), OWID (World coal+oil, CC BY 4.0), OWID-legacy + DECC/BEIS (UK coal 1700-2019). Tests Wen (2005)'s central claim: Kitchin survives on sectoral series (inventory, coal, pig iron, rail freight) not on macro composites. Per-variable Gate 1 (Roadmap #14 safeguard) directly applicable.

datasets.name: DECC/BEIS UK coal 1700-2019 (via OWID legacy GitHub mirror)

variable_codes.notes: US Wholesale Price Index — splice NBER General Price Level (1860-1939) + BLS PPIACO (1913-2026). Juglar's original variable in 1862.
US Industrial Production Index (FRB G.17) — 1919-2026 monthly. Modern reference for industrial-cycle testing.
US Total Coal Production — annual 1856-1958. Wen (2005)'s prototypical inventory-cycle variable.
US Steel Ingots Production — splice annual 1863-1919 + monthly 1932-1965. Schumpeter (1939) Juglar driver.
US Pig Iron Production (Iron Age/AISI) — monthly 1941-1964. Limited span but a canonical Kitchin inventory variable.
US Railroad Freight Ton-Miles (Babson) — monthly 1866-1922. Kitchin (1923) original.
US Wheat Production — annual 1866-1952. Agricultural inventory cycle.
US Cotton Mill Consumption — monthly 1912-1961. Textile inventory cycle.
UK Coal Output (DECC/BEIS) — annual 1913-2019 (decadal 1700-1900). 107 annual observations post-1913.
